economic_notes: Multiple commercial enterprises including hydroelectric power (25% of Kwagis Power LP - 41.5MW run-of-river facility), sand and gravel (12% of Orca Sand and Gravel LP), forestry (Atli Resources LP with 20-year forest license expiring 2034, Danyas Forest Products LP 49.5% interest), land-based salmon aquaculture (Kuterra LP - ceased operations Feb 2020, facility leased to third party), fishing licences (Mama'omas LP), real estate (NBDC Real Estate LP), and North Island Driving School Society. Fishing license surplus revenues placed in reserve fund.
notes: Fiscal year end is March 31, 2023 (not 2024). Nation is based in Alert Bay, BC. Consolidated statements include 'Namgis First Nation Government, Trust Funds, Social Housing Programs, Holding Company Ltd., and Business Development Corporation. Twelve commercial enterprises accounted for using modified equity method (GBEs). Prior year figures are restated per Note 17. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($11,052,159) + FNHA ($5,079,933) + Province of BC ($5,061,142) + Fisheries & Oceans ($3,510,432) + CMHC ($1,745,394) + Other Federal Grants ($1,650,000) + FNESC ($478,722) + Municipal services ($123,599) + NVIATS ($95,051) + Licence lease ($81,946) = $28,878,378; however share of business enterprise income ($5,495,686) is equity pickup not a government transfer. OSR includes interest income, sales, rental income, contributions from related entities, and other. Detailed program expense schedules exist across 120+ pages but individual totals were not fully extracted from the provided text. Note 17 restatement of prior year figures noted but not detailed in provided text.
